title: 10 Settlement and Reports
body_text:
10	Settlement and Reports
10.1	Settlement of Return Items and Reimbursement Claims
Canadian dollar cheque returns are credited to your financial institution芒聙聶s Canadian dollar current account. US dollar returns are credited to your financial institution芒聙聶s US dollar current account.
Note:	Reimbursement for government items will be credited to your financial institution芒聙聶s current account once funds are received from the provincial or federal government.
10.2	Online Return Processed Items Report (ORPI/ORUI)
Description
The Online Return Processed Items report is known as the ORPI for CAD Online Return Processed Items, and as the ORUI for USD Online Return Processed Items. Because these reports are structured in a similar fashion, the information in this Section applies to both reports. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: ORPI / ORUI Report
Purpose
The ORPI/ORUI report summarizes returned cheques, including reinstated or image-excluded cheques, and manually returned cheques. The ORPI is for CAD cheques, and the ORUI is for USD cheques.
Review the ORPI and the ORUI reports daily to verify that all requested returns were processed. Notify Central 1 immediately if a discrepancy is identified.
Organization
The top portion of the report summarizes individual cheque returns and reinstated and image-excluded cheques. The middle portion summarizes the totals by each item type. The bottom portion is the settlement date and total settlement that will be posted to your financial institution芒聙聶s current account.
Distribution
Returns will appear in the appropriate report in the following day芒聙聶s MERG file, which can be downloaded from Central 1芒聙聶s File and Report Exchange. MERG files are in your financial institution芒聙聶s root directory. If applicable, PDFs of the ORPI and ORUI reports are available in the Clearing folder. Go to the 芒聙聹CAD芒聙聺 subfolder to access Canadian dollar reports and the 芒聙聹USD芒聙聺 subfolder for US dollar reports. Refer to the File Exchange and Report Distribution Part for more information on accessing the File and Report Exchange.
10.3	Account Activity and Balances Report 芒聙聯 AIBC/AIBU (BC), AIOC/AIOU (Ontario)
Description
The Account Activity and Balances report provides a daily summary of all transactions posted to your financial institution芒聙聶s current account.
Canadian dollar transactions are reported on the AIBC (for BC) or the AIOC (for Ontario). US dollar transactions are reported on the AIBU (for BC) or the AIOU (for Ontario). Because these reports are structured in a similar fashion, the information in this Section applies to all four reports. See Figure 2. The information in this report is also available on CAS Online. Refer to the Current Account Services (CAS) Online Part for information on accessing CAS Online.
Figure 2: AIBC Report
Purpose
Financial institutions can use this report to confirm postings of return totals.
Organization
If your financial institution has multiple Charters, the branches associated with each charter can be reported on a separate page. For example, if your financial institution has two charters, 163 and 162, the branches under Charter 163 (16310, 16320, 16330) can be reported on one page. The branches under Charter 162 (16210, 16220, 16230) can be reported on another.
The top section of the report lists the previous, opening, and closing balances, and the net activity. The middle section lists the postings by branch and by type. Items with RI as the Transaction Code are return items. The lower section summarizes the total debit and credit postings to each branch.
Distribution
This report is produced on Business Days and is available to financial institutions the following day.
The AIBC/AIBU (for BC) is in the root directory. The AIOC/AIOU (for Ontario) is available in the ONDL file that is in the root directory. If applicable, PDFs of these reports are available in the Banking folder. Refer to the File Exchange and Report Distribution Part for more information on accessing the File and Report Exchange.
END
